Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Master bowyer Tom Turgeon joins George Vondriska in the workshop to show you how to make your own long-bow. They'll discuss the kits available on the market and demystify the terminology used when talking about bow making. Learn about the art and intricac
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Making a Long-Bow in Your Shop

Image.alt: Making a Long-Bow in Your Shop
Making a Long-Bow in Your Shop
Making a Long-Bow in Your Shop

Video.summary: Master carver George Johnson joins George Vondriska in the workshop to share his time-honored carving knowledge and show you how to make your own handcrafted holiday ornament. George demonstrates the intricate art of carving as he brings a wooden Santa-shaped ornament to life. This project makes for a thoughtful gift or a one-of-a-kind adornment for your tree.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Hand-Carved Santa Ornament

Video.summary: With a few supplies and cold connection techniques, designer Abbi Berta quickly creates mixed media earrings. She also makes a textured cuff with a torch-fired patina to introduce metalwork. Viewer Leslie Rocha tells how her first jewelry class and love of music blossomed into a passion for making jewelry from guitar wire and picks.
Video.title: Getting Started in Jewelry Making

Video.summary: Angela raids her husband's closet to stitch up a special quilted Father's Day gift. This T-shirt quilt for Jeremy isn't just any old T-shirt quilt; it's a very cool design that uses big flying geese to frame the blocks.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Don't be overwhelmed interpreting designs and styles. Learn how to narrow down design choices by imposing some limits. Ebony Love shows how to limit your palette when placing color. Then, Catherine Cureton has a pieced applique technique that leaves no raw edges and uses a sewing machine. Eliane Bergmann has a lesson on seams in the machine tip. Learn how to make the perfect Ľ" seam and how to stitch a partial seam.
Video.title: Limits

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: The Walters household is eating healthy this week, but that doesn't mean Angela's quilts have to be. Today she is whipping up the Milky Way Quilt by Monique Dillard featuring Lily & Loom fabric.
Video.title: Milky Way

Video.summary: In the Season 5 finale of The Midnight Quilt show, Angela is creating a modern quilt of her own design that involves a 36-patch block!
Video.title: Modern X

Video.summary: Angela is kicking off Season 7 of the Midnight Quilt Show with a redemption story. She’s made this Thistle quilt pattern before with half-square triangles but thinks this flying-geese version will be much easier to quilt.
Video.title: Flying Geese Quilt Redo!

Video.summary: Angela is back! In addition to quilting an amazing half-square triangle quilt by Monique Dillard, she's also announcing something HUGE!
Video.title: Night Sky Quilt

Video.summary: Quilting just got better. Join Angela in her first episode as she stitches up her exclusive variable-star quilt design.
Video.title: Stars

Video.summary: What do you get when you combine a rainbow quilt, a star quilt and a medallion quilt all into one? A fresh episode of "The Midnight Quilt Show," of course! Join Angela as she breaks down Y-seams, quilting with templates and beyond.
Video.title: Frequency Rainbow Star Quilt

Video.summary: What do you get when you combine a rainbow quilt, a star quilt and a medallion quilt all into one? A fresh episode of "The Midnight Quilt Show," of course! Join Angela as she breaks down Y-seams, quilting with templates and beyond.
Video.title: Frequency Rainbow Star Quilt

Video.summary: What happens when a rail fence meets a log cabin? Angela Walters' modern, lush batik quilt, demonstrated here!
Video.title: Rail Fence

Video.summary: As the new century dawned, fashion had gone from corsets to spandex pants in just 100 years. Even dogs were getting dressed up. Paulette Bell creates a customized dog coat using machine embroidery. Angela Wolf adds a hand warmer to work-out wear for today's trendy touch.
Video.title: The New Millennium - the 2000's

Video.summary: Nations raced to put satellites into space and the fashion scene took a futuristic step toward figure-hugging fashions. Gretchen Hirsch shows how to redesign a pattern to make a trendy blouse that buttons up the back and designer Peggy Sagers takes a cue from a man's shirt to create a flattering shirtwaist dress with easy collar variations.
Video.title: The Space Race Begins - The Late 1950's

Image.alt: The Space Race Begins - The Late 1950's
The Space Race Begins - The Late 1950's
The Space Race Begins - The Late 1950's

Video.summary: Securely finish your jewelry with flair. Jewelry designer and blogger Molly Schaller returns with techniques using crimps and other findings to secure closures. In the Beading Lesson, Katie makes personalized bangles from 16-gauge wire and crystals that tell your story.
Video.title: Close It Up

Video.summary: Stephanie Eddy shares some of her secrets about Viking knitting -- making a bracelet and ways to add beads to Viking knit. Katie has some special tips for making stretchy bracelets more durable in her Beading Lesson.
Video.title: Learn How to Viking Knit

Video.summary: See how new machines are making their mark on your scrapbook page. Kazan Clark creates a stencil with a die cut machine and then shows how to etch on glass. Julie interviews Jenny Barnett Rohrs about how social media inspires crafting. Then, Joe Rotella uses computer software to create a snowflake Christmas ornament. Julie wraps things up with a lesson on design featuring a scrapbook page picturing family members.
Video.title: Episode 4: Technology

Video.summary: This episode features paint and ink techniques. First, Dina Wakley uses paint and ink to create a vibrant watercolor background for her page layout. Julie interviews Joe Rotella about his glass etching project. Then, Kathy Cano Murillo tie dyes fabric to use as a background for her page layout. Last, Julie creates a tie dye look with a watercolor technique.
Video.title: Episode 12: Paint and Ink

Video.summary: An all-encompassing show for anyone who loves to make things, from mixed media to metalsmithing.
Video.title: Make it Artsy

Video.summary: Make your art sparkle. Artist Jen Cushman wraps wire and stamps words to create a metallic flower. Host Julie Fei-Fan Balzer crafts a variety of projects with decorative foils. Artist and teacher Cat Kerr molds shining eyes to use in lots of different projects. Learn a new tip from Jane Dunnewold.
Video.title: Shiny

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Since when do knitters use crochet hooks? Find out how to work the provisional cast-on, cable cast-on, and more during the first Novel-T check in!
Video.title: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Image.alt: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Video.summary: Having trouble with your Fox Paws scarf? Well, you're not the only one! Luckily, designer Xandy is here to share her tips and tricks for navigating her famously intricate pattern.
Video.title: Stacked Increases + FOX PAWS Knitting Pattern

Image.alt: Stacked Increases + FOX PAWS Knitting Pattern
Stacked Increases + FOX PAWS Knitting Pattern
Stacked Increases + FOX PAWS Knitting Pattern

Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Craft Hacks

Video.summary: We are on the move, with projects that go along with us, or move themselves! Join host Katie Hacker and make a rockin' roadster that really rolls. Artist Franz Spohn uses a little string to add animation to his art and blogger Jenny Barnett Rohrs make a wreath with colorful felt pinwheels. Use the Family Craft Box and recycled denim to make a car track. Decorate your room with an eye-catching string art project that uses color to create the feeling of movement.
Video.title: On the Move

Video.summary: Let's head outside for some fun. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs makes colorful hand grips for your bike. Katie Hacker has some fun with friendly bugs. Franz Spohn has sidewalk art tips and Katie makes special discovery eggs.
Video.title: Too Nice to Stay Inside

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Artist's Academy presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: What do you do when your daughter picks out a quilt that's way harder than you're used to? Well if you're like Angela, you make an episode about it! This one's all about building up the confidence to take a bite out of something new. (You can do it too!)
Video.title: Episode 1
